expectations.text: Code imports addUpdateLead from scripts/lib/influencersoft.mjs (the project's existing client lib, not raw fetch)
expectations.evidence: Option 1 and 2 both import addUpdateLead from './lib/influencersoft.mjs' — the project's existing helper, not a raw fetch implementation.

expectations.text: add_tags string includes exactly 'customer:etsy' (the trigger tag for post-purchase-etsy-buyer sequence — case-sensitive, colon-separated)
expectations.evidence: add_tags: 'customer:etsy,source:etsy,...' appears in both Option 1 and Option 2. Exact string, correct case.

expectations.text: add_tags string includes a product tag in the format 'product:tax-001-mileage-log' (lowercase, hyphenated SKU slug — not 'TAX-001' or 'product:TAX-001')
expectations.evidence: product:tax-001-mileage-log present in skuMap and tags array. Correct format: lowercase, hyphenated slug.

expectations.text: Answer mentions or references tag-dictionary.md as the source of truth for tag strings
expectations.evidence: JSDoc comment '// Map SKU to product tag per tag-dictionary.md §3' and Tag Reference section cites 'infrastructure/influencersoft/tag-dictionary.md (121 lines, authoritative)'.

expectations.text: Answer references the tenant 'kebron' or the URL kebron.influencersoft.com somewhere (so the reader knows which tenant the call hits)
expectations.evidence: n8n section shows POST URL 'https://kebron.influencersoft.com/api/AddUpdateLead' and API Key section references IS_TENANT=kebron.

expectations.text: Answer does NOT log the full POST body or rpsKey (security: key leakage risk; the lib enforces this but the answer should respect it too)
expectations.evidence: Console.log calls only emit email and success message. No POST body, no rpsKey logged anywhere.
